title: Calystegia macounii
intro: Calystegia macounii, also known as Macoun's morning glory, is a perennial vine native to the western regions of North America, characterized by its twining stems and trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may aid in relieving symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Bioactive constituents of Calystegia macounii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Calystegia macounii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications for its purported anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
taxonomy: Calystegia macounii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It belongs to the genus Calystegia, which is a group of morning glories native to the Northern Hemisphere. Calystegia macounii is further categorized as a member of the order Solanales and the clade Asterids. The plant's classification places it within the kingdom Plantae, indicating its status as a vascular plant. Its specific classification provides insight into the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the Convolvulaceae family.
